ai:cl.c: Manages network connections via TCP/IP sockets.

ai:a.c: Streams raw data to the socket for writing.
ai:a.p: ERR acDataFeed(*Object, OBJECTID Object, DATA Datatype, APTR Buffer, INT Size)

ai:i.c: Must refer to the unique ID of the object that you represent. If you do not represent an object, set this parameter to the current task ID.
The type of data being sent.
The data being sent to the target object.
The size of the data in Buffer.

ai:a.c: Disables sending and receiving on the socket.

ai:i.c: A mutable buffer that will receive the data.
The Read action will write this parameter with the total number of bytes read into the Buffer.

ai:a.p: ERR acWrite(*Object, std::span<const int8_t> Buffer, INT *Result)

ai:i.c: A buffer containing the data that will be written to the object.
This parameter with be updated with the total number of bytes written from the Buffer.

ai:m.c: Connects a NetSocket to an address.
ai:m.p: ERR ns::Connect(OBJECTPTR Object, STRVIEW Address, INT Port, DOUBLE Timeout)

ai:i.c: String containing either a domain name (e.g. www.google.com) or an IP address (e.g. 123.123.123.123)
Remote port to connect to.
Connection timeout in seconds (0 = no timeout).

ai:m.c: Returns the IP address that the socket is locally bound to.
ai:m.p: ERR ns::GetLocalIPAddress(OBJECTPTR Object, struct IPAddress * Address)

ai:i.c: Pointer to an IPAddress structure which will be set to the result of the query if successful.

ai:m.c: Join a multicast group for receiving multicast packets (UDP only).

ai:i.c: The multicast group address to join (e.g. 224.1.1.1).
The multicast group address to leave.

ai:m.c: Receive a datagram packet from any address (UDP only).
ai:m.p: ERR ns::RecvFrom(OBJECTPTR Object, struct IPAddress * Source, std::span<int8_t> Buffer, INT * BytesRead)

ai:i.c: Source IP address of the received packet.
Output buffer for received data.
Number of bytes actually received.

ai:m.c: Send a datagram packet to a specific address (UDP only).
ai:m.p: ERR ns::SendTo(OBJECTPTR Object, struct IPAddress * Dest, std::span<const int8_t> Data, INT * BytesSent)

ai:i.c: The destination IP address (IPv4 or IPv6) and port number.
Pointer to the data buffer to send.
Number of bytes actually sent.

ai:f.c: An IP address or domain name to connect to.
A client-defined value that can be useful in action notify events.
Information about the last error that occurred during a NetSocket operation
A callback trigger for when the state of the NetSocket is changed.
Platform specific reference to the network socket handle.
Callback that is triggered when the socket receives data.
Maximum UDP packet size for sending and receiving data.
Limits the size of incoming and outgoing data packets.
The number of bytes on the socket's outgoing queue.
Callback that is triggered when a socket is ready to send data.
The port number to use for connections.
The current connection state of the NetSocket object.
